title: Self-contained proof of hook super-Schur formula s_{(a,1^b)}(q/u)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma G5.** Lemma `ef{lem:schur-1-1}` is currently delegated entirely to \cite{ChengWangBook}. For a self-contained solution, derive from the definition
$$s_\lambda(q/u)=\sum_{
u\subseteq\lambda}s_
u(q)\,s_{\lambda'/
u'}(u)$$
(single commuting variable $q$, single anticommuting variable $u$) that:

- $s_{(a,1^b)}(q/u)=q^a u^b+q^{a-1}u^{b+1}$ for $a\ge1,\ b\ge0$;
- $s_\varnothing(q/u)=1$;
- $s_\lambda(q/u)=0$ whenever $\lambda$ is **not** a hook.

**Approach.** In one variable, $s_
u(q)
e0$ iff $
u$ is a single row $(
u=(r))$, with $s_{(r)}(q)=q^r$; a single-variable skew Schur $s_{\mu/
u}(u)
e0$ iff $\mu/
u$ is a single column (horizontal-strip-free vertical strip), equal to $u^{|\mu/
u|}$. Enumerate the two surviving $
u\subseteq(a,1^b)$ for which both factors are nonzero, and show all non-hook $\lambda$ have no surviving $
u$.

This is a short computation (a few lines) but should be present rather than cited, to make the hook-coefficient extraction (G2) self-contained.
